title: Character vs. Word Tokenization
concept: Word tokenization is intuitive but suffers from large vocabulary sizes and out-of-vocabulary words; character tokenization has tiny vocabularies but makes sequences extremely long.

exercises.prompt: What are tokens in the context of AI?

options.text: The pieces of text that an AI processes
Secret passwords used to secure AI models

exercises.explain: Tokens are the building blocks of text for an AI, created by breaking sentences down into smaller units like words or characters.

cards.title: How AI Reads
cards.body: Before an AI can read your messages, it has to chop the text into bite-sized pieces called tokens. But should it chop by whole words or letter-by-letter? Let's find out!

cards.title: Splitting Text
cards.body: Tokenization is how we chop text into smaller pieces (tokens) for AI to understand. **Word Tokenization** splits text by spaces into whole words. **Character Tokenization** cuts text into individual letters and symbols.

cards.title: The Lego Analogy
cards.body: Think of a Lego castle. Word tokenization is like using pre-made toy rooms (quick to build, but you need a giant toy box to store every room type). Character tokenization is like using single, tiny Lego bricks (you only need a few colors, but it takes forever to build a house brick-by-brick).

cards.title: See it in Action
cards.body:
Take the sentence "Hey Cat!". 

• **Word Tokenization** splits it into 2 pieces: ["Hey", "Cat!"]. Easy to read, but the AI must learn thousands of unique words. 

• **Character Tokenization** splits it into 8 pieces: ["H", "e", "y", " ", "C", "a", "t", "!"]. Tiny vocabulary, but the sequence becomes very long!

cards.title: The Typo Trap
cards.body: Many people think word tokenization is always the best because it is how humans read. However, if a user types a typo like "greast", a word tokenizer gets completely stuck because it has never seen that "word" before!

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Here is how the word "Robot" is treated by both methods. Notice the trade-off between vocabulary size (how many unique tokens exist) and sequence length (how many pieces we get).

left.text: Output: ["Robot"] (1 token). Big vocabulary needed, but short sequence.

right.text: Output: ["R", "o", "b", "o", "t"] (5 tokens). Tiny vocabulary, but long sequence.

exercises.prompt: What is the main drawback of using character tokenization?

options.text: It makes the vocabulary size way too large.
It makes the text sequences extremely long.
It cannot handle typos or new words.

exercises.explain: Character tokenization splits text into individual letters, meaning a short sentence like "Hello" becomes 5 separate tokens: H-e-l-l-o. This makes the sequences much longer for the AI to process.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Word tokenization results in a smaller vocabulary size than character tokenization.
exercises.explain: Word tokenization requires storing thousands of unique words, whereas character tokenization only needs to store the letters of the alphabet and basic symbols.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank with the correct tokenization type.
exercises.sentence: Because it splits text into individual letters, ________ tokenization results in the smallest vocabulary size.
exercises.explain: Character tokenization chops text into single letters, resulting in a very small vocabulary (just the alphabet and punctuation) but very long sequences.

exercises.prompt: Identify the main limitation of word tokenization:

options.text: It splits words into tiny, meaningless single letters.
It struggles with out-of-vocabulary words and typos.
It makes sequences too long for the AI to read.

exercises.explain: Word tokenization is fast and intuitive because it matches how we think of words, but it fails when it encounters brand-new words or typos that aren't in its dictionary.

exercises.prompt: Your AI model needs to process a text filled with internet slang and typos like 'hoolllaaa' and 'puzzzled'. Why is word tokenization a poor choice here?

options.text: Word tokenization creates much longer text sequences for the AI to read.
Word tokenization cannot handle unknown words or typos easily.
Word tokenization requires a tiny, easy-to-manage vocabulary size.
Word tokenization breaks words down into individual letters.

exercises.explain: Word tokenization struggles with typos and new words because they aren't in its pre-made vocabulary list. Character tokenization doesn't have this problem because it can spell out any word letter-by-letter.
